title: Pentaphyllon lupinaster
taxonomy: Pentaphyllon lupinaster is classified within the family Pentaphyllaceae. This family is an ancient group of plants, with some estimates suggesting it dates back to the Cretaceous period. Pentaphyllon lupinaster is an evergreen shrub native to South Africa. The classification of Pentaphyllon lupinaster at the genus and species levels is distinct, reflecting its unique characteristics. Further classification details may be found within the broader taxonomic hierarchy of the plant kingdom.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation. side_effects: Pentaphyllon lupinaster, also known as the woodbine, is generally considered non-toxic and safe for humans and animals. intro: Pentaphragm lupinaster, commonly known as the Lungwort or Pentaphyllon lupinaster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Boraginaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Pentaphragm lupinaster include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support lung health and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
